12.32: Do not be afraid, little flock, for your? Father {Pat?r} is pleased to give you? the kingdom.
12.33: Sell your? possessions and give to the poor. Provide yourselves with purses (money bag) that will not wear out, an inexhaustible treasure in heaven, where no thief approaches and no moth destroys.
12.34: For where your? treasure is, there your? heart will be also.
12.35: Be dressed for service and keep your? lamps burning.
12.36: Then you? will be like servants waiting for their master {kyrion} to return from the wedding banquet, so that when he comes and knocks, they can open the door for him at once.
12.37: Blessed are those servants {douloi} whom the master {kyrios} finds on watch when he returns. Truly I tell you?, he will dress himself to serve and will have them recline at the table, and he himself will come and wait on them.
12.38: Even if he comes in the second or third watch of the night (between nine at night and three in the morning) and finds them alert, those servants will be blessed.
12.39: But understand this: If the homeowner had known at what hour the thief was coming [he would have stayed awake, and], he would not have let his house be broken into.
12.40: You? also must be ready, because the Son of Man will come at an hour you? do not expect.
12.41: “Lord {Kyrie}, said Peter (rock), “are You addressing this parable to us, or to everyone else as well?
12.42: And the Lord {Kyrios} answered, “Who then is the faithful and wise manager, whom the master puts in charge of his servants to give them their portion at the proper time?
12.43: Blessed is that servant {doulos} whose master {kyrios} finds him doing so when he returns.
12.44: Truly I tell you?, he will put him in charge of all his possessions.
12.45: But suppose that servant {doulos} says in his heart, ‘My master {kyrios} will be a long time in coming, and he begins to beat the menservants and maidservants, and to eat and drink and get drunk.
12.46: The master {kyrios} of that servant {doulou} will come on a day he does not expect and at an hour he does not anticipate. Then he will cut him to pieces and assign him a place with the unbelievers.
12.47: That servant {doulos} who knows his master’s {kyriou} will but does not get ready or follow his instructions will be beaten with many blows.
12.48: But the one who unknowingly does things worthy of punishment will be beaten with few blows. From everyone who has been given much, much will be required; and from him who has been entrusted with much, even more will be demanded.
12.49: I have come to ignite a fire on the earth, and how I wish it were already kindled!
12.50: But I have a baptism (submersion) to undergo, and how distressed I am until it is accomplished!
12.51: Do you? think that I have come to bring peace to the earth? No, I tell you?, but division.
12.52: From now on, five in one household will be divided, three against two and two against three.
12.53: They will be divided, father {pat?r} against son and son against father, mother against daughter and daughter against mother, mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law .
12.54: Then Jesus said to the crowds, “As soon as you? see a cloud rising in the west, you? say, ‘A shower is coming, and that is what happens.
12.55: And when the south wind blows, you? say, ‘It will be hot, and it is.
12.56: You hypocrites! You? know how to interpret the appearance of the earth and sky. Why don’t you? know how to interpret the present time?
12.57: And why don’t you? judge for yourselves what is right?
12.58: Make every effort to reconcile with your adversary while you are on your way to the magistrate. Otherwise, he may drag you off to the judge, and the judge may hand you over to the officer, and the officer may throw you into prison.
12.59: I tell you, you will not get out until you have paid the very last penny {lepton}.

13.1: At that time some of those present told Jesus about the Galileans whose blood Pilate (armed with a spear) had mixed with their sacrifices.
13.2: To this He replied, “Do you? think that these Galileans were worse sinners than all the other Galileans, because they suffered this way?
13.3: No, I tell you?. But unless you? repent, you? too will all perish.
13.4: Or those eighteen who were killed when the tower of Siloam collapsed on them: Do you? think that they were more sinful than all the others living in Jerusalem (city of peace)?
13.5: No, I tell you?. But unless you? repent, you? too will all perish.
13.6: Then Jesus told this parable: “A man had a fig tree that was planted in his vineyard. He went to look for fruit on it but did not find any.
13.7: So he said to the keeper of the vineyard, ‘Look (Behold), for the past three years I have come to search for fruit on this fig tree and haven’t found any. Therefore cut it down [Cut it down!]! Why should it use up the soil?
13.8: ‘Sir {Kyrie}, the man replied, ‘leave it alone again this year, until I dig around it and fertilize it.
13.9: If it bears fruit next year, fine. But if not, you can cut it down.
13.10: One Sabbath Jesus was teaching {didask?n} in one of the synagogues,
13.11: and a woman there had been disabled by a spirit {pneuma} for eighteen years. She was hunched over and could not stand up straight.
13.12: When Jesus (YHWH saves) saw her, He called her over and said, “Woman, you are set free from your disability.
13.13: Then He placed His hands on her, and immediately she straightened up and began to glorify God {Theon}.
13.14: But the synagogue leader (ruler) was indignant that Jesus (YHWH saves) had healed on the Sabbath. “There are six days for work, he told the crowd. “So come and be healed on those days and not on the Sabbath.
13.15: “You hypocrites! the Lord {Kyrios} replied. “Does not each of you? on the Sabbath untie his ox or donkey from the stall and lead it to water?
13.16: Then should not this daughter of Abraham (father of many), whom Satan (the adversary) has kept bound for eighteen long years, be released from her bondage on the Sabbath day?
13.17: When Jesus said this, all His adversaries were humiliated. And the whole crowd rejoiced at all the glorious things He was doing.
13.18: Then Jesus asked, “What is the kingdom of God {Theou} like? To what can I compare it?
13.19: It is like a mustard seed that a man tossed into his garden. It grew and became a tree, and the birds of the air nested in its branches.
13.20: Again He asked, “To what can I compare the kingdom of God {Theou}?
13.21: It is like leaven that a woman took and mixed into three measures of flour, until all of it was leavened.
13.22: Then Jesus traveled throughout the towns and villages, teaching {didask?n} as He made His way toward Jerusalem (city of peace).
13.23: “Lord {Kyrie}, someone asked Him, “will only a few people be saved? Jesus answered,
13.24: “Make every effort to enter through the narrow door. For many, I tell you?, will try to enter and will not be able.
13.25: After the master of the house gets up and shuts the door, you? will stand outside knocking and saying, ‘Lord {Kyrie}, open the door for us. But he will reply, ‘I do not know where you? are from.
13.26: Then you? will say, ‘We ate and drank with you, and you taught in our streets.
13.27: And he will answer, ‘I tell you?, I do not know where you? are from. Depart from me, all you evildoers.
13.28: There will be weeping and gnashing (grinding) of teeth when you? see Abraham (father of many), Isaac (he will laugh), Jacob (he grasps the heel), and all the prophets in the kingdom of God {Theou}, but you? yourselves are thrown out.
13.29: People will come from east and west and north and south, and will recline at the table in the kingdom of God {Theou}.
13.30: And indeed, some who are last will be first, and some who are first will be last.
13.31: At that very hour, some Pharisees came to Jesus and told Him, “Leave this place and get away, because Herod (heroic) wants to kill You.
13.32: But Jesus replied, “Go tell that fox, ‘Look (Behold), I will keep driving out demons and healing people today and tomorrow, and on the third day I will reach My goal.
13.33: Nevertheless, I must keep going today and tomorrow and the next day, for it is not admissible for a prophet to perish outside of Jerusalem (city of peace).
13.34: O Jerusalem (city of peace), Jerusalem, who kills the prophets and stones those sent to her, how often I have longed to gather your children together as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, but you? were unwilling!
13.35: Look (Behold), your? house is left to you? desolate. And I tell you? that you? will not see Me again until you? say, ‘Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord .’

14.1: One Sabbath, Jesus went to eat in the home of a leading Pharisee, and those in attendance were watching Him closely.
14.2: Right there before Him was a man with dropsy.
14.3: So Jesus (YHWH saves) asked the experts in the law and the Pharisees, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath or not?
14.4: But they remained silent. Then Jesus took hold of the man, healed him, and sent him on his way.
14.5: And He asked them, “Which of you? whose son [donkey] or ox falls into a pit on the Sabbath day will not immediately pull him out?
14.6: And they were unable to answer these questions.
14.7: When Jesus noticed how the guests chose the places of honor, He told them a parable:
14.8: “When you are invited to a wedding banquet, do not sit in the place of honor, in case someone more distinguished than you has been invited.
14.9: Then the host who invited both of you will come and tell you, ‘Give this man your seat. And in humiliation, you will have to take the last place.
14.10: But when you are invited, go and sit in the last place, so that your host will come and tell you, ‘Friend, move up to a better place . Then you will be honored {doxa} in front of everyone at the table with you.
14.11: For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and the one who humbles himself will be exalted.
14.12: Then Jesus said to the man who had invited Him, “When you host a dinner or a banquet, do not invite your friends or brothers or relatives or rich neighbors. Otherwise, they may invite you in return, and you will be repaid.
14.13: But when you host a banquet, invite the poor, the crippled, the lame, and the blind,
14.14: and you will be blessed. Since they cannot repay you, you will be repaid at the resurrection (rising again) of the righteous.
14.15: When one of those reclining with Him heard this, he said to Jesus, “Blessed is everyone who will eat at the feast \who will eat bread/ in the kingdom of God {Theou}.
14.16: But Jesus replied, “A certain man prepared a great banquet and invited many guests.
14.17: When it was time for the banquet, he sent his servant {doulon} to tell those who had been invited, ‘Come, for everything is now ready.
14.18: But one after another they all began to make excuses. The first one said, ‘I have bought a field, and I need to go see it. Please excuse me.
14.19: Another said, ‘I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I am going to try them out. Please excuse me.
14.20: Still another said, ‘I have married a wife, so I cannot come.
14.21: The servant {doulos} returned and reported all this to his master {kyri?}. Then the owner of the house became angry and said to his servant {doul?}, ‘Go out quickly into the streets and alleys of the city, and bring in the poor, the crippled, the blind, and the lame.
14.22: ‘Sir {Kyrie}, the servant {doulos} replied, ‘what you ordered has been done, and there is still room.
14.23: So the master {kyrios} told his servant {doulon}, ‘Go out to the highways and hedges and compel them to come in, so that my house will be full.
14.24: For I tell you?, not one of those men who were invited will taste my banquet.
14.25: Now large crowds were traveling with Jesus, and He turned and said to them,
14.26: “If anyone comes to Me and does not hate his father {patera} and mother and wife and children and brothers and sisters—yes, even his own life {psych?n}—he cannot be My disciple (pupil).
14.27: And whoever does not carry his cross and follow Me cannot be My disciple (pupil).
14.28: Which of you?, wishing to build a tower, does not first sit down and count the cost to see if he has the resources to complete it?
14.29: Otherwise, if he lays the foundation and is unable to finish the work, everyone who sees it will ridicule him,
14.30: saying, ‘This man could not finish what he started to build.
14.31: Or what king on his way to war with another king will not first sit down and consider whether he can engage with ten thousand men the one coming against him with twenty thousand?
14.32: And if he is unable, he will send a delegation while the other king is still far off, to ask for terms of peace.
14.33: In the same way, any one of you? who does not give up everything he has cannot be My disciple (pupil).
14.34: Salt is good, but if the salt loses its savor, with what will it be seasoned?
14.35: It is fit neither for the soil nor for the manure pile, and it is thrown out. He who has ears to hear, let him hear.

15.1: Now all the tax collectors and sinners were gathering around to listen to Jesus.
15.2: So the Pharisees and scribes began to grumble: “This man welcomes sinners and eats with them.
15.3: Then Jesus told them this parable:
15.4: “What man among you?, if he has a hundred sheep and loses one of them, does not leave the ninety-nine in the pasture and go after the one that is lost, until he finds it?
15.5: And when he finds it, he joyfully puts it on his shoulders,
15.6: comes home, and calls together his friends and neighbors to tell them, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my lost sheep!
15.7: I tell you? that in the same way there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous ones who do not need to repent.
15.8: Or what woman who has ten silver coins \ten drachmas/ and loses one of them does not light a lamp, sweep her house, and search carefully until she finds it?
15.9: And when she finds it, she calls together her friends and neighbors to say, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my lost coin.
15.10: In the same way, I tell you?, there is joy in the presence of God’s {Theou} angels (messengers) over one sinner who repents.
15.11: Then Jesus said, “There was a man who had two sons.
15.12: The younger son said to him, ‘Father {Pater}, give me my share of the estate. So he divided his property between them.
15.13: After a few days, the younger son got everything together and journeyed to a distant country, where he squandered his wealth in wild living.
15.14: After he had spent all he had, a severe famine swept through that country, and he began to be in need.
15.15: So he went and hired himself out to a citizen of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed the pigs.
15.16: He longed to fill his belly with the pods the pigs were eating, but no one would give him a thing.
15.17: Finally he came to his senses and said, ‘How many of my father’s hired servants have plenty of food, but here I am, starving to death!
15.18: I will get up and go back to my father {patera} and say to him, “Father {Pater}, I have sinned against heaven and against you.
15.19: I am no longer worthy to be called your son. Make me like one of your hired servants.
15.20: So he got up and went to his father {patera}. But while he was still in the distance, his father {pat?r} saw him and was filled with compassion. He ran to his son, embraced him, and kissed him.
15.21: The son declared, ‘Father {Pater}, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I am no longer worthy to be called your son [Make me like one of your hired servants].
15.22: But the father {pat?r} said to his servants {doulous}, ‘Quick! Bring the best robe and put it on him. Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet.
15.23: Bring the fattened calf and kill it. Let us feast and celebrate.
15.24: For this son of mine was dead and is alive again! He was lost and is found! So they began to celebrate.
15.25: Meanwhile the older son was in the field, and as he approached the house, he heard music and dancing.
15.26: So he called one of the servants and asked what was going on.
15.27: ‘Your brother has returned, he said, ‘and your father {pat?r} has killed the fattened calf, because he has him back safe and sound.
15.28: The older son became angry and refused to go in. So his father {pat?r} came out and pleaded with him.
15.29: But he answered his father, ‘Look (Behold), all these years I have served you and never disobeyed a commandment of yours. Yet you never gave me even a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.
15.30: But when this son of yours returns from squandering your wealth with prostitutes, you kill the fattened calf for him!
15.31: ‘Son, you are always with me, the father said, ‘and all that is mine is yours?.
15.32: But it was fitting to celebrate and be glad, because this brother of yours was dead and is alive again; he was lost and is found.

16.1: Jesus also said to His disciples (pupils), “There was a rich man whose manager was accused of wasting his possessions.
16.2: So he called him in to ask, ‘What is this I hear about you? Turn in an account of your management, for you cannot be manager any longer.
16.3: The manager said to himself, ‘What shall I do, now that my master {kyrios} is taking away my position? I am too weak to dig and too ashamed to beg.
16.4: I know what I will do so that after my removal from management, people will welcome me into their homes.
16.5: And he called in each one of his master’s {kyriou} debtors. ‘How much do you owe my master {kyri?}? he asked the first.
16.6: ‘A hundred measures of olive oil <870 gal / 3,300 l>, he answered. ‘Take your bill, said the manager, ‘sit down quickly, and write fifty.
16.7: Then he asked another, ‘And how much do you owe? ‘A hundred measures of wheat <1,000 bu / 35,000 l>, he replied. ‘Take your bill and write eighty, he told him.
16.8: The master {kyrios} commended the dishonest manager because he had acted shrewdly. For the sons of this age are more shrewd in dealing with their own kind than are the sons of light.
16.9: I tell you?, use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves so that when it is gone, they will welcome you? into eternal dwellings.
16.10: Whoever is faithful with very little will also be faithful with much, and whoever is dishonest with very little will also be dishonest with much.
16.11: So if you? have not been faithful with worldly wealth, who will entrust you? with true riches?
16.12: And if you? have not been faithful with the belongings of another, who will give you? belongings of your own?
16.13: No servant can serve two masters {kyriois}. Either he will hate the one and love {agap?sei} the other, or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You? cannot serve both God {The?} and money.
16.14: The Pharisees, who were lovers of money, heard all of this and were scoffing at Jesus.
16.15: So He said to them, “You? are the ones who justify yourselves before men, but God {Theos} knows your? hearts. For what is prized among men is detestable before God {Theou}.
16.16: The Law and the Prophets were proclaimed until John (YHWH is gracious). Since that time, the gospel of the kingdom of God {Theou} is being preached, and everyone is forcing his way into it (everyone is urged to enter into it).
16.17: But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for a single stroke of a pen to drop out of the Law.
16.18: Anyone who divorces his wife and marries another woman commits adultery, and he who marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
16.19: Now there was a rich man dressed in purple and fine linen, who lived each day in joyous splendor.
16.20: And a beggar named Lazarus (God has helped) lay at his gate, covered with sores
16.21: and longing to be fed with the crumbs that fell from the rich man’s table. Even the dogs came and licked his sores.
16.22: One day the beggar died and was carried by the angels (messengers) to Abraham’s side \into Abraham’s bosom/. And the rich man also died and was buried.
16.23: In Hades, where he was in torment, he looked up and saw Abraham (father of many) from afar, with Lazarus (God has helped) by his side.
16.24: So he cried out, ‘Father {Pater} Abraham (father of many), have mercy on me and send Lazarus (God has helped) to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue (language). For I am in agony in this fire.
16.25: But Abraham (father of many) answered, ‘Child, remember that during your lifetime you received your good things, while Lazarus (God has helped) received bad things. But now he is comforted here, while you are in agony.
16.26: And besides all this, a great chasm has been fixed between us and you?, so that even those who wish cannot cross from here to you?, nor can anyone cross from there to us.
16.27: ‘Then I beg you, father {pater}, he said, ‘send Lazarus to my father’s house,
16.28: for I have five brothers. Let him warn them, so that they will not also end up in this place of torment.
16.29: But Abraham (father of many) replied, ‘They have Moses (drawn out) and the Prophets; let your brothers listen to them.
16.30: ‘No, father {pater} Abraham (father of many), he said, ‘but if someone is sent to them from the dead, they will repent.
16.31: Then Abraham said to him, ‘If they do not listen to Moses (drawn out) and the Prophets, they will not be persuaded even if someone rises from the dead.

17.1: Jesus said to His disciples (pupils), “It is inevitable that stumbling blocks will come, but woe to the one through whom they come!
17.2: It would be better for him to have a millstone hung around his neck and to be thrown into the sea than to cause one of these little ones to stumble.
17.3: Watch yourselves. If your brother sins, rebuke him; and if he repents, forgive him.
17.4: Even if he sins against you seven times in a day, and seven times returns to say, ‘I repent, you must forgive him.
17.5: The apostles (sent ones) said to the Lord {Kyri?}, “Increase our faith!
17.6: And the Lord {Kyrios} answered, “If you? have faith the size of a mustard seed, you? can say to this mulberry tree, ‘Be uprooted and planted in the sea, and it will obey you?.
17.7: Which of you? whose servant {doulon} comes in from plowing or shepherding in the field will say to him, ‘Come at once and sit down to eat’
17.8: Instead, won’t he tell him, ‘Prepare my meal and dress yourself to serve me while I eat and drink, and afterward you may eat and drink’?
17.9: Does he thank the servant {doul?} because he did what he was told?
17.10: So you? also, when you? have done everything commanded of you?, should say, ‘We are unworthy servants {Douloi}; we have only done our duty.
17.11: While Jesus was on His way to Jerusalem (city of peace), He was passing between Samaria and Galilee (circuit).
17.12: As He entered one of the villages, He was met by ten lepers . They stood at a distance
17.13: and raised their voices, shouting, “Jesus (YHWH saves), Master, have mercy on us!
17.14: When Jesus saw them, He said, “Go, show yourselves to the priests . And as they were on their way, they were cleansed.
17.15: When one of them saw that he was healed, he came back, praising God {Theon} in a loud voice.
17.16: He fell facedown at Jesus’ feet in thanksgiving to Him—and he was a Samaritan.
17.17: “Were not all ten cleansed? Jesus (YHWH saves) asked. “Where then are the other nine?
17.18: Was no one found except this foreigner to return and give glory {doxan} to God {The?}?
17.19: Then Jesus said to him, “Rise and go; your faith has made you well (has saved you)!
17.20: When asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God {Theou} would come, Jesus replied, “The kingdom of God will not come with observable signs.
17.21: Nor will people say, ‘Look (Behold), here it is, or ‘There it is. For you see, the kingdom of God {Theou} is in your? midst (within you).
17.22: Then He said to the disciples (pupils), “The time is coming when you? will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you? will not see it.
17.23: People will tell you?, ‘Look (Behold), there He is! or ‘Look, here He is! Do not go out or chase after them.
17.24: For just as the lightning flashes and lights up the sky from one end to the other, so will be the Son of Man in His day.
17.25: But first He must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
17.26: Just as it was in the days of Noah (comfort), so also will it be in the days of the Son of Man:
17.27: People were eating and drinking, marrying and being given in marriage, up to the day Noah (comfort) entered the ark. Then the flood came and destroyed them all.
17.28: It was the same in the days of Lot (covering): People were eating and drinking, buying and selling, planting and building.
17.29: But on the day Lot (covering) left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
17.30: It will be just like that on the day the Son of Man is revealed.
17.31: On that day, let no one on the housetop come down to retrieve his possessions. Likewise, let no one in the field return for anything he has left behind.
17.33: Whoever tries to save his life {psych?n} will lose it, but whoever loses his life will preserve it.
17.34: I tell you?, on that night two people will be in one bed: one will be taken and the other left.
17.35: Two women will be grinding grain together: one will be taken and the other left [36 Two men will be in the field. One will be taken and the other left].”
17.37: “Where, Lord {Kyrie}? they asked. Jesus answered, “Wherever there is a carcass, there the vultures will gather.

18.1: Then Jesus told them a parable about their need to pray at all times and not lose heart:
18.2: “In a certain town there was a judge who neither feared God {Theon} nor respected men.
18.3: And there was a widow in that town who kept appealing to him, ‘Give me justice against my adversary.
18.4: For a while he refused, but later he said to himself, ‘Though I neither fear God {Theon} nor respect men,
18.5: yet because this widow keeps pestering me, I will give her justice. Otherwise, she will wear me out with her perpetual requests.
18.6: And the Lord {Kyrios} said, “Listen to the words of the unjust judge.
18.7: Will not God {Theos} bring about justice for His elect who cry out to Him day and night? Will He delay in helping them?
18.8: I tell you?, He will promptly carry out justice on their behalf. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will He find faith on earth?
18.9: To some who trusted in their own righteousness and viewed others with contempt, He also told this parable:
18.10: “Two men went up to the temple to pray. One was a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
18.11: The Pharisee stood by himself and prayed (stood and prayed to himself), ‘God {Theos}, I thank You that I am not like other men—swindlers, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.
18.12: I fast twice a week and pay tithes of all that I acquire.
18.13: But the tax collector stood at a distance, unwilling even to lift up his eyes to heaven. Instead, he beat his breast and said, ‘God {Theos}, have mercy on me, a sinner!
18.14: I tell you?, this man, rather than the Pharisee, went home justified. For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but the one who humbles himself will be exalted.
18.15: Now people were even bringing their babies to Jesus for Him to place His hands on them. And when the disciples (pupils) saw this, they rebuked those who brought them.
18.16: But Jesus (YHWH saves) called the children to Him and said, “Let the little children come to Me, and do not hinder them! For the kingdom of God {Theou} belongs to such as these.
18.17: Truly I tell you?, anyone who does not receive the kingdom of God {Theou} like a little child will never enter it.
18.18: Then a certain ruler asked Him, “Good Teacher {Didaskale}, what must I do to inherit eternal life?
18.19: “Why do you call Me good? Jesus (YHWH saves) replied. “No one is good except God {Theos} alone.
18.20: You know the commandments: ‘Do not commit adultery, do not murder, do not steal, do not bear false witness, honor your father {patera} and mother .’
18.21: “All these I have kept from my youth, he said.
18.22: On hearing this, Jesus (YHWH saves) told him, “You still lack one thing: Sell everything you own and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow (accompany) Me.
18.23: But when the ruler heard this, he became very sad, because he was extremely wealthy.
18.24: Seeing the man’s sadness \Seeing that he had become sorrowful/, Jesus (YHWH saves) said, “How hard it is for the rich to enter the kingdom of God {Theou}!
18.25: Indeed, it is easier for a camel to pass through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God {Theou}.
18.26: Those who heard this asked, “Who then can be saved?
18.27: But Jesus said, “What is impossible with man is possible with God {The?}.
18.28: “Look (Behold), said Peter (rock), “we have left all we had [left all] to follow You.
18.29: “Truly I tell you?, Jesus replied, “no one who has left home or wife or brothers or parents or children for the sake of the kingdom of God {Theou}
18.30: will fail to receive many times more in this age—and in the age to come, eternal life.
18.31: Then Jesus took the Twelve aside and said to them, “Look (Behold), we are going up to Jerusalem (city of peace), and everything the prophets have written about the Son of Man will be fulfilled.
18.32: He will be delivered over to the Gentiles {ethnesin} and will be mocked and insulted and spit upon.
18.33: They will flog Him and kill Him, and on the third day He will rise again.
18.34: But the disciples did not understand any of these things. The meaning {rh?ma} was hidden from them, and they did not comprehend what He was saying.
18.35: As Jesus drew near to Jericho (city of palms), a blind man was sitting beside the road, begging.
18.36: When he heard the crowd going by, he asked what was happening.
18.37: “Jesus (YHWH saves) of Nazareth (branch) is passing by, they told him.
18.38: So he called out, “Jesus (YHWH saves), Son of David (beloved), have mercy on me!
18.39: Those who led the way admonished him to be silent, but he cried out all the louder, “Son of David (beloved), have mercy on me!
18.40: Jesus (YHWH saves) stopped and directed that the man be brought to Him. When he had come near, Jesus asked him,
18.41: “What do you want Me to do for you? “Lord {Kyrie}, he said, “let me see again.
18.42: “Receive your sight! Jesus (YHWH saves) replied. “Your faith has healed you.
18.43: Immediately he received his sight and followed Jesus, glorifying God {Theon}. And all the people, when they saw it, gave praise to God {The?}.

19.1: Then Jesus entered Jericho (city of palms) and was passing through.
19.2: And there was a man named Zacchaeus, a chief tax collector, who was very wealthy.
19.3: He was trying to see who Jesus (YHWH saves) was, but could not see over the crowd because he was small in stature.
19.4: So he ran on ahead and climbed a sycamore tree to see Him, since Jesus was about to pass that way.
19.5: When Jesus came to that place, He looked up and said, “Zacchaeus, hurry down, for I must stay at your house today.
19.6: So Zacchaeus hurried down and welcomed Him joyfully.
19.7: And all who saw this began to grumble, saying, “He has gone to be the guest of a sinful man!
19.8: But Zacchaeus stood up and said to the Lord {Kyrion}, “Look (Behold), Lord {Kyrie}, half of my possessions I give to the poor, and if I have cheated anyone, I will repay it fourfold.
19.9: Jesus (YHWH saves) said to him, “Today salvation has come to this house, because this man too is a son of Abraham (father of many).
19.10: For the Son of Man came to seek and to save the lost.
19.11: While the people were listening to this, Jesus proceeded to tell them a parable, because He was near Jerusalem (city of peace) and they thought the kingdom of God {Theou} would appear imminently.
19.12: So He said, “A man of noble birth went to a distant country to lay claim to his kingship and then return.
19.13: Beforehand, he called ten of his servants {doulous} and gave them ten minas (he gave each servant one mina). ‘Conduct business with this until I return, he said.
19.14: But his subjects hated him and sent a delegation after him to say, ‘We do not want this man to rule over us.
19.15: When he returned from procuring his kingship, he summoned the servants {doulous} to whom he had given the money (silver), to find out what each one had earned.
19.16: The first servant came forward and said, ‘Master {Kyrie}, your mina has produced ten more minas.
19.17: His master replied, ‘Well done, good servant {doule}! Because you have been faithful in a very small matter, you shall have authority over ten cities.
19.18: The second servant came and said, ‘Master {kyrie}, your mina has made five minas.
19.19: And to this one he said, ‘You shall have authority over five cities.
19.20: Then another servant came and said, ‘Master {Kyrie}, here is your mina, which I have laid away in a piece of cloth {soudari?}.
19.21: For I was afraid of you, because you are a harsh man. You withdraw what you did not deposit and reap what you did not sow.
19.22: His master replied, ‘You wicked servant {doule}, I will judge you by your own words. So you knew that I am a harsh man, withdrawing what I did not deposit and reaping what I did not sow?
19.23: Why then did you not deposit my money (silver) in the bank, and upon my return I could have collected it with interest?
19.24: Then he told those standing by, ‘Take the mina from him and give it to the one who has ten minas.
19.26: He replied, ‘I tell you? that everyone who has will be given more; but the one who does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.
19.27: And these enemies of mine who were unwilling for me to rule over them, bring them here and slay them in front of me.
19.28: After Jesus had said this, He went on ahead, going up to Jerusalem (city of peace).
19.29: As He approached Bethphage (house of figs) and Bethany (house of affliction) at the Mount of Olives, He sent out two of His disciples (pupils),
19.30: saying, “Go into the village ahead of you, and as you enter it, you? will find a colt tied there, on which no one has ever sat. Untie it and bring it here.
19.31: If anyone asks, ‘Why are you? untying it?’ tell him, ‘The Lord {Kyrios} needs it.
19.32: So those who were sent went out and found it just as Jesus had told them.
19.33: As they were untying the colt, its owners {kyrioi} asked, “Why are you? untying the colt?
19.35: Then they led the colt to Jesus (YHWH saves), threw their cloaks over it, and put Jesus on it.
19.36: As He rode along, the people spread their cloaks on the road.
19.37: And as He approached the descent from the Mount of Olives, the whole multitude of disciples (pupils) began to praise God {Theon} joyfully in a loud voice for all the miracles they had seen:
19.38: “Blessed is the King who comes in the name of the Lord !” “Peace in heaven and glory {doxa} in the highest !”
19.39: But some of the Pharisees in the crowd said to Him, “Teacher {Didaskale}, rebuke Your disciples (pupils)!
19.40: “I tell you?, He answered, “if they remain silent, the very stones will cry out.
19.41: As Jesus approached Jerusalem and saw the city, He wept over it
19.42: and said, “If only you had known on this day what would bring you peace! But now it is hidden from your eyes.
19.43: For the days will come upon you when your enemies will barricade you and surround you and hem you in on every side.
19.44: They will level you to the ground—you and the children within your walls. They will not leave one stone on another, because you did not recognize the time of your visitation from God \your visitation/.
19.45: Then Jesus entered the temple courts \the temple/ and began to drive out those who were selling there.
19.46: He declared to them, “It is written: ‘My house will be a house of prayer .’ But you? have made it ‘a den of robbers .’
19.47: Jesus was teaching {didask?n} at the temple every day, but the chief priests, scribes, and leaders of the people were intent on killing Him.
19.48: Yet they could not find a way to do so, because all the people hung on His words.

20.1: One day as Jesus was teaching {didaskontos} the people in the temple courts \the temple/ and proclaiming the gospel (good news), the chief priests and scribes, together with the elders, came up to Him.
20.2: “Tell us, they said, “by what authority are You doing these things, and who gave You this authority?
20.3: “I will also ask you? a question, Jesus replied. “Tell Me:
20.4: John’s (YHWH is gracious) baptism (submersion)—was it from heaven, or from men?
20.5: They deliberated among themselves and said, “If we say, ‘From heaven, He will ask, ‘Why did you? not believe (have faith) him?
20.6: But if we say, ‘From men, all the people will stone us, for they are convinced that John (YHWH is gracious) was a prophet.
20.7: So they answered that they did not know where it was from.
20.8: And Jesus (YHWH saves) replied, “Neither will I tell you? by what authority I am doing these things.
20.9: Then He proceeded to tell the people this parable: “A man planted a vineyard, rented it out to some tenants (farmers), and went away for a long time.
20.10: At harvest time, he sent a servant {doulon} to the tenants (farmers) to collect his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants beat the servant and sent him away empty-handed.
20.11: So he sent another servant {doulon}, but they beat him and treated him shamefully, sending him away empty-handed.
20.12: Then he sent a third, but they wounded him and threw him out.
20.13: ‘What shall I do? asked the owner {kyrios} of the vineyard. ‘I will send my beloved {agap?ton} son. Perhaps they will respect him.
20.14: But when the tenants (farmers) saw the son, they discussed it among themselves and said, ‘This is the heir. Let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.
20.15: So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What then will the owner {kyrios} of the vineyard do to them?
20.16: He will come and kill those tenants (farmers) and give the vineyard to others. And when the people heard this, they said, “May such a thing never happen!
20.17: But Jesus looked directly at them and said, “Then what is the meaning of that which is written: ‘The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone ’
20.18: Everyone who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces, but he on whom it falls will be crushed.
20.19: When the scribes and chief priests realized that Jesus had spoken this parable against them, they sought to arrest Him that very hour. But they were afraid of the people.
20.20: So they watched Him closely and sent spies who pretended to be sincere. They were hoping to catch Him in His words in order to hand Him over to the rule and authority of the governor.
20.21: “Teacher {Didaskale}, they inquired, “we know that You speak and teach {didaskeis} correctly. You show no partiality but teach the way of God {Theou} in accordance with the truth.
20.22: Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to Caesar (Emporer) or not?
20.23: But Jesus saw through their duplicity and said to them,
20.24: “Show Me a denarius (day’s wage). Whose image and inscription are on it? “Caesar’s, they answered.
20.25: So Jesus told them, “Give to Caesar (Emporer) what is Caesar’s, and to God {The?} what is God’s {Theou}.
20.26: And they were unable to trap Him in His words {rh?matos} before the people. And amazed at His answer, they fell silent.
20.27: Then some of the Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection (rising again), came to question Him.
20.28: “Teacher {Didaskale}, they said, “Moses (drawn out) wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies and leaves a wife but no children, the man is to marry his brother’s widow and raise up offspring (seed) for him .
20.29: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a wife but died childless.
20.30: Then the second [married the widow, and he also died,]
20.31: and the third married the widow, and in the same way all seven died, leaving no children.
20.32: And last of all, the woman died.
20.33: So then, in the resurrection (rising again), whose wife will she be? For all seven were married to her.
20.34: Jesus (YHWH saves) answered, “The sons of this age marry and are given in marriage.
20.35: But those who are considered worthy to share in the age to come and in the resurrection (rising again) from the dead will neither marry nor be given in marriage.
20.36: In fact, they can no longer die, because they are like the angels. And since they are sons of the resurrection (rising again), they are sons of God {Theou}.
20.37: Even Moses (drawn out) demonstrates that the dead are raised, in the passage about the burning bush. For he calls the Lord {Kyrion} ‘the God {Theon} of Abraham (father of many), the God of Isaac (he will laugh), and the God of Jacob .’
20.38: He is not the God {Theos} of the dead, but of the living, for to Him all are alive.
20.39: Some of the scribes answered, “Teacher {Didaskale}, You have spoken well!
20.40: And they did not dare to question Him any further.
20.41: Then Jesus declared, “How can it be said that the Christ (the Anointed One) is the Son of David (beloved)?
20.42: For David (beloved) himself says in the book (papyrus roll) of Psalms: ‘The Lord {Kyrios} said to my Lord {Kyri?}, “Sit at My right hand
20.43: until I make Your enemies a footstool for Your feet .”’
20.44: Thus David (beloved) calls Him ‘Lord {Kyrion}. So how can He be David’s son?
20.45: In the hearing of all the people, Jesus said to His disciples (pupils),
20.46: “Beware of the scribes. They like to walk around in long robes, and they love {philount?n} the greetings in the marketplaces (town square), the chief seats in the synagogues, and the places of honor at banquets.
20.47: They defraud widows of their houses \They devour widows’ houses/, and for a show make lengthy prayers. These men will receive greater condemnation.

21.1: Then Jesus looked up and saw the rich putting their gifts into the treasury,
21.2: and He saw a poor widow put in two small copper coins \two lepta/.
21.3: “Truly I tell you?, He said, “this poor widow has put in more than all the others.
21.4: For they all contributed out of their surplus, but she out of her poverty has put in all she had to live on.
21.5: As some of the disciples were remarking how the temple was adorned with beautiful stones and consecrated gifts, Jesus said,
21.6: “As for what you? see here, the time will come when not one stone will be left on another; every one will be thrown down.
21.7: “Teacher {Didaskale}, they asked, “when will these things happen? And what will be the sign that they are about to take place?
21.8: Jesus answered, “See to it that you? are not deceived. For many will come in My name, claiming, ‘I am He, and, ‘The time is near. Do not follow them.
21.9: When you? hear of wars and rebellions, do not be alarmed. These things must happen first, but the end is not imminent.
21.10: Then He told them, “Nation {ethnos} will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.
21.11: There will be great earthquakes, famines, and pestilences in various places, along with fearful sights and great signs from heaven.
21.12: But before all this, they will seize you? and persecute you. On account of My name they will deliver you to the synagogues and prisons, and they will bring you before kings and governors.
21.13: This will be your? opportunity to serve as witnesses.
21.14: So make up your? mind not to worry beforehand how to defend yourselves.
21.15: For I will give you? speech and wisdom that none of your? adversaries will be able to resist or contradict.
21.16: You? will be betrayed even by parents and brothers and relatives and friends, and some of you? will be put to death.
21.17: And you? will be hated by everyone because of My name.
21.18: Yet not even a hair of your? head will perish.
21.19: By your? patient endurance (perseverance) you? will gain your? souls {psychas}.
21.20: But when you? see Jerusalem (city of peace) surrounded by armies, you? will know that her desolation is near.
21.21: Then let those who are in Judea (land of praise) flee to the mountains, let those in the city get out, and let those in the country stay out of the city.
21.22: For these are the days of vengeance, to fulfill all that is written.
21.23: How miserable those days will be for pregnant and nursing mothers! For there will be great distress upon the land and wrath against this people.
21.24: They will fall by the edge of the sword and be led captive into all the nations {ethn?}. And Jerusalem (city of peace) will be trodden down by the Gentiles {ethn?n}, until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.
21.25: There will be signs in the sun and moon and stars, and on the earth dismay among the nations {ethn?n}, bewildered by the roaring of the sea and the surging of the waves.
21.26: Men will faint from fear and anxiety over what is coming upon the earth, for the powers {dynameis} of the heavens will be shaken.
21.27: At that time they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power {dyname?s} and great glory .
21.28: When these things begin to happen, stand up and lift up your? heads, because your? redemption is drawing near.
21.29: Then Jesus told them a parable: “Look at (behold) the fig tree and all the trees.
21.30: When they sprout leaves, you? can see for yourselves and know that summer is near.
21.31: So also, when you? see these things happening, know that the kingdom of God {Theou} is near.
21.32: Truly I tell you?, this generation will not pass away until all these things have happened.
21.33: Heaven and earth will pass away, but My words will never pass away.
21.34: But watch yourselves, or your? hearts will be weighed down by dissipation, drunkenness, and the worries of life—and that day will spring upon you? suddenly like a snare.
21.35: For it will come upon all who dwell on the face of all the earth.
21.36: So keep watch at all times, and pray that you? may have the strength to escape all that is about to happen and to stand before the Son of Man.
21.37: Every day Jesus taught {didask?n} at the temple, but every evening He went out to spend the night on the Mount of Olives.
21.38: And early in the morning all the people would come to hear Him at the temple.

22.1: Now the Feast of Unleavened Bread \the feast of the Unleavened/, called the Passover, was approaching,
22.2: and the chief priests and scribes were looking for a way to put Jesus to death, for they feared the people.
22.3: Then Satan (the adversary) entered Judas (praise) Iscariot, who was one of the Twelve.
22.4: And Judas went to discuss with the chief priests and temple officers how he might betray Jesus to them.
22.5: They were delighted and agreed to give him money (silver).
22.6: Judas consented, and began to look for an opportunity to betray Jesus to them in the absence of a crowd.
22.7: Then came the day of Unleavened Bread on which the Passover lamb was to be sacrificed.
22.8: Jesus sent Peter (rock) and John (YHWH is gracious), saying, “Go and make preparations for us to eat the Passover.
22.9: “Where do You want us to prepare it? they asked.
22.10: He answered, “When you? enter the city, a man carrying a jug of water will meet you?. Follow (accompany) him to the house he enters,
22.11: and say to the owner of that house, ‘The Teacher {Didaskalos} asks: Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with My disciples (pupils)?
22.12: And he will show you? a large upper room, already furnished. Make preparations there.
22.13: So they went and found it just as Jesus had told them. And they prepared the Passover.
22.14: When the hour had come, Jesus reclined at the table with His apostles (sent ones).
22.15: And He said to them, “I have eagerly desired to eat this Passover with you? before My suffering.
22.16: For I tell you? that I will not eat it again until it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God {Theou}.
22.17: After taking the cup, He gave thanks and said, “Take this and divide it among yourselves.
22.18: For I tell you? that I will not drink of the fruit of the vine from now on until the kingdom of God {Theou} comes.
22.19: And He took the bread, gave thanks and broke it, and gave it to them, saying, “This is My body, given for you?; do this in remembrance of Me.
22.20: In the same way, after supper He took the cup, saying, “This cup is the new covenant {diath?k?} in My blood, which is poured out for you?.
22.21: Look (Behold)! The hand of My betrayer is with Mine on the table.
22.22: Indeed, the Son of Man will go as it has been determined, but woe to that man who betrays Him.
22.23: Then they began to question among themselves which of them was going to do this.
22.24: A dispute also arose among the disciples as to which of them should be considered the greatest.
22.25: So Jesus declared, “The kings of the Gentiles {ethn?n} lord it over them, and those in authority over them call themselves benefactors.
22.26: But you? shall not be like them. Instead, the greatest among you? should be like the youngest, and the one who leads like the one who serves {diakon?n}.
22.27: For who is greater, the one who reclines at the table or the one who serves {diakon?n}? Is it not the one who reclines? But I am among you? as one who serves.
22.28: You? are the ones who have stood by Me in My trials.
22.29: And I bestow on you? a kingdom, just as My Father {Pat?r} has bestowed one on Me,
22.30: so that you? may eat and drink at My table in My kingdom and sit on thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel (He wrestles with God).
22.31: Simon (hearing), Simon, Satan (the adversary) has asked to sift all of you? like wheat.
22.32: But I have prayed for you, Simon, that your faith will not fail. And when you have turned back, strengthen your brothers.
22.33: “Lord {Kyrie}, said Peter, “I am ready to go with You even to prison and to death.
22.34: But Jesus replied, “I tell you, Peter (rock), the rooster will not crow today until you have denied three times that you know Me.
22.35: Then Jesus asked them, “When I sent you? out without purse (money bag) or bag or sandals, did you? lack anything? “Nothing, they answered.
22.36: “Now, however, He told them, “the one with a purse (money bag) should take it, and likewise a bag; and the one without a sword should sell his cloak and buy one.
22.37: For I tell you? that this Scripture must be fulfilled in Me: ‘And He was numbered with the transgressors .’ For what is written about Me is reaching its fulfillment.
22.38: So they said, “Look (Behold), Lord {Kyrie}, here are two swords. “That is enough, He answered.
22.39: Jesus went out as usual to the Mount of Olives, and the disciples (pupils) followed Him.
22.40: When He came to the place, He told them, “Pray that you will not enter into temptation.
22.41: And He withdrew about a stone’s throw beyond them, where He knelt down and prayed,
22.42: “Father {Pater}, if You are willing, take this cup from Me. Yet not My will, but Yours be done.
22.43: Then an angel (messenger) from heaven appeared to Him and strengthened Him.
22.44: And in His anguish, He prayed more earnestly, and His sweat became like drops of blood falling to the ground.
22.45: When Jesus rose from prayer and returned to the disciples (pupils), He found them asleep, exhausted from sorrow.
22.46: “Why are you? sleeping? He asked. “Get up and pray so that you? will not enter into temptation.
22.47: While He was still speaking, a crowd arrived, led by the man called Judas (praise), one of the Twelve. He approached Jesus (YHWH saves) to kiss Him.
22.48: But Jesus (YHWH saves) asked him, “Judas (praise), are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?
22.49: Those around Jesus saw what was about to happen and said, “Lord {Kyrie}, should we strike with our swords?
22.50: And one of them struck the servant {doulon} of the high priest, cutting off his right ear.
22.51: But Jesus (YHWH saves) answered, “No more of this! And He touched the man’s ear and healed him.
22.52: Then Jesus (YHWH saves) said to the chief priests, temple officers, and elders who had come for Him, “Have you? come out with swords and clubs as you would against an outlaw?
22.53: Every day I was with you? in the temple courts \the temple/, and you? did not lay a hand on Me. But this hour belongs to you? and to the power of darkness.
22.54: Then they seized Jesus, led Him away, and took Him into the house of the high priest. And Peter (rock) followed at a distance.
22.55: When those present had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter (rock) sat down among them.
22.56: A servant girl saw him seated in the firelight and looked intently at him. “This man also was with Him, she said.
22.57: But Peter denied it. “Woman, I do not know Him, he said.
22.58: A short time later, someone else saw him and said, “You also are one of them. But Peter (rock) said, “Man, I am not.
22.59: About an hour later, another man insisted, “Certainly this man was with Him, for he too is a Galilean.
22.60: “Man, I do not know what you are talking about, Peter (rock) replied. And immediately, while he was still speaking, the rooster crowed.
22.61: And the Lord {Kyrios} turned and looked at Peter (rock). Then Peter remembered the word that the Lord {Kyriou} had spoken to him: “Before the rooster crows today, you will deny Me three times.
22.62: And he went outside and wept bitterly.
22.63: The men who were holding Jesus began to mock Him and beat Him.
22.64: They blindfolded Him [and were striking Him on the face.] and kept demanding, “Prophesy! Who hit You?
22.65: And they said many other blasphemous things against Him.
22.66: At daybreak the council of the elders of the people, both the chief priests and scribes, met together. They led Jesus (YHWH saves) into their Sanhedrin (their Council) and said,
22.67: “If You are the Christ (the Anointed One), tell us. Jesus answered, “If I tell you?, you? will not believe.
22.68: And if I ask you a question, you? will not answer.
22.69: But from now on the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power {dyname?s} of God .”
22.70: So they all asked, “Are You then the Son of God {Theou}? He replied, “You? say that I am.
22.71: “Why do we need any more testimony? they declared. “We have heard it for ourselves from His own lips.

23.1: Then the whole council rose and led Jesus away to Pilate (armed with a spear).
23.2: And they began to accuse Him, saying, “We found this man subverting our nation {ethnos}, forbidding payment of taxes to Caesar (Emporer), and proclaiming Himself to be Christ (the Anointed One), a King.
23.3: So Pilate (armed with a spear) asked Him, “Are You the King of the Jews (people of praise)? “You have said so, Jesus replied.
23.4: Then Pilate (armed with a spear) said to the chief priests and the crowds, “I find no basis for a charge against this man.
23.5: But they kept insisting, “He stirs up the people all over Judea (land of praise) with His teaching {didask?n}. He began in Galilee (circuit) and has come all the way here.
23.6: When Pilate (armed with a spear) heard this, he asked if the man was a Galilean.
23.7: And learning that Jesus was under Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent Him to Herod (heroic), who himself was in Jerusalem (city of peace) at that time.
23.8: When Herod (heroic) saw Jesus (YHWH saves), he was greatly pleased. He had wanted to see Him for a long time, because he had heard about Him and was hoping to see Him perform a miracle.
23.9: Herod questioned Jesus at great length, but He gave no answer.
23.10: Meanwhile, the chief priests and scribes stood there, vehemently accusing Him.
23.11: And even Herod (heroic) and his soldiers ridiculed and mocked Him. Dressing Him in a fine robe, they sent Him back to Pilate (armed with a spear).
23.12: That day Herod (heroic) and Pilate (armed with a spear) became friends; before this time they had been enemies.
23.13: Then Pilate (armed with a spear) called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people,
23.14: and said to them, “You? brought me this man as one who was inciting the people to rebellion. I have examined Him here in your? presence and found Him not guilty of your? charges against Him.
23.15: Neither has Herod (heroic), for he sent Him back to us. As you can see (Behold), He has done nothing deserving of death.
23.16: Therefore I will punish Him and release Him.” [17 Now Pilate was obligated to release to the people one prisoner at the feast.]
23.18: But they all cried out in unison: “Away with this man! Release Barabbas (Son of Abba) to us!
23.19: (Barabbas had been imprisoned for an insurrection in the city, and for murder.)
23.20: Wanting to release Jesus (YHWH saves), Pilate (armed with a spear) addressed them again,
23.21: but they kept shouting, “Crucify Him! Crucify Him!
23.22: A third time he said to them, “What evil has this man done? I have found in Him no offense worthy of death. So after I punish Him, I will release Him.
23.23: But they were insistent, demanding with loud voices for Jesus to be crucified. And their clamor [and that of the chief priests.] prevailed.
23.24: So Pilate (armed with a spear) sentenced that their demand be met.
23.25: As they had requested, he released the one imprisoned for insurrection and murder, and he handed Jesus (YHWH saves) over to their will.
23.26: As the soldiers led Him away, they seized Simon (hearing) of Cyrene on his way in from the country, and they put the cross on him to carry behind Jesus (YHWH saves).
23.27: A great number of people followed Him, including women who kept mourning and wailing for Him.
23.28: But Jesus (YHWH saves) turned to them and said, “Daughters of Jerusalem (city of peace), do not weep for Me, but weep for yourselves and for your? children.
23.29: Look (Behold), the days are coming when people will say, ‘Blessed are the barren women, the wombs that never bore, and breasts that never nursed!
23.30: At that time ‘they will say to the mountains, “Fall on us! and to the hills, “Cover us !”’
23.31: For if men do these things while the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry?
23.32: Two others, who were criminals, were also led away to be executed with Jesus.
23.33: When they came to the place called The Skull {Kranion}, they crucified Him there, along with the criminals, one on His right and the other on His left.
23.34: Then Jesus (YHWH saves) said, “Father {Pater}, forgive them, for they do not know what they are doing.” And they divided up His garments by casting lots .
23.35: The people stood watching, and the rulers sneered at Him , saying, “He saved others; let Him save Himself if He is the Christ (the Anointed One) of God {Theou}, the Chosen One.
23.36: The soldiers also mocked Him and came up to offer Him sour wine (to offer Him wine vinegar).
23.37: “If You are the King of the Jews (people of praise), they said, “save Yourself!
23.38: Above Him was posted an inscription [written in Greek, Latin, and Hebrew]: THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS (people of praise).
23.39: One of the criminals who hung there heaped abuse on Him. “Are You not the Christ (the Anointed One)? he said. “Save Yourself and us!
23.40: But the other one rebuked him, saying, “Do you not even fear God {Theon}, since you are under the same judgment?
23.41: We are punished justly, for we are receiving what our actions deserve. But this man has done nothing wrong.
23.42: Then he said, “Jesus (YHWH saves), remember me [said to Jesus, “Remember me, Lord,] when You come into Your kingdom!
23.43: And Jesus said to him, “Truly I tell you, today you will be with Me in Paradise.
23.44: It was now about the sixth hour, and darkness came over all the land until the ninth hour (from noon until 3 pm).
23.45: The sun was darkened [became dark], and the veil of the temple was torn down the middle.
23.46: Then Jesus (YHWH saves) called out in a loud voice, “Father {Pater}, into Your hands I commit My Spirit .” And when He had said this, He breathed His last.
23.47: When the centurion (commander of a hundred) saw what had happened, he gave glory {edoxazen} to God {Theon}, saying, “Surely this was a righteous man (an innocent man).
23.48: And when all the people who had gathered for this spectacle saw what had happened, they returned home beating their breasts.
23.49: But all those who knew Jesus, including the women who had followed Him from Galilee (circuit), stood at a distance watching these things.
23.50: Now there was a Council member named Joseph (he will add), a good and righteous man,
23.51: who had not consented to their decision or action. He was from the Judean town of Arimathea (High place) and was waiting for the kingdom of God {Theou}.
23.52: He went to Pilate (armed with a spear) to ask for the body of Jesus (YHWH saves).
23.53: Then he took it down, wrapped it in a linen cloth, and placed it in a tomb cut into the rock, where no one had yet been laid.
23.54: It was Preparation Day, and the Sabbath was beginning (was about to begin).
23.55: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee (circuit) followed, and they saw the tomb and how His body was placed.
23.56: Then they returned to prepare spices and perfumes. And they rested on the Sabbath, according to the commandment.

24.1: On the first day of the week \But on the first of the Sabbaths,/, very early in the morning, the women came to the tomb, bringing the spices they had prepared.
24.2: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb,
24.3: but when they entered, they did not find the body of the Lord {Kyriou} Jesus (YHWH saves).
24.4: While they were puzzling over this, suddenly (Behold) two men in radiant apparel stood beside them.
24.5: As the women bowed their faces to the ground in terror, the two men asked them, “Why do you? look for the living among the dead?
24.6: He is not here; He has risen! Remember how He told you? while He was still in Galilee (circuit):
24.7: ‘The Son of Man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified, and on the third day rise again.
24.9: And when they returned from the tomb, they reported all these things to the Eleven and to all the others.
24.10: It was Mary (beloved) Magdalene (of Magdala), Joanna, Mary the mother of James (supplanter / follower), and the other women with them who told this to the apostles (sent ones).
24.11: But their words {rh?mata} seemed like nonsense to them, and they did not believe the women.
24.12: Peter (rock), however, got up and ran to the tomb. And after bending down and seeing only the linen cloths, he went away, wondering to himself what had happened.
24.13: That same day two of them were going to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles <6.9 mi / 11.1 km> from Jerusalem (city of peace).
24.14: They were talking with each other about everything that had happened.
24.15: And as they talked and deliberated, Jesus (YHWH saves) Himself came up and walked along with them.
24.16: But their eyes were kept from recognizing Him.
24.17: He asked them, “What are you? discussing so intently as you walk along? They stood still, with sadness on their faces.
24.18: One of them, named Cleopas, asked Him, “Are You the only visitor to Jerusalem (city of peace) who does not know the things that have happened there in recent days?
24.19: “What things? He asked. “The events involving Jesus (YHWH saves) of Nazareth (branch), they answered. “This man was a prophet, powerful in speech and action before God {Theou} and all the people.
24.20: Our chief priests and rulers delivered Him up to the sentence of death, and they crucified Him.
24.21: But we were hoping He was the One who would redeem Israel (He wrestles with God). And besides all this, it is the third day since these things took place.
24.22: Furthermore, some of our women astounded us. They were at the tomb early this morning,
24.23: but they did not find His body. They came and told us they had seen a vision of angels (messengers), who said that Jesus was alive.
24.24: Then some of our companions went to the tomb and found it just as the women had described. But Him they did not see.
24.25: Then Jesus said to them, “O foolish ones, and slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken!
24.26: Was it not necessary for the Christ (the Anointed One) to suffer these things and then to enter His glory {doxan}?
24.27: And beginning with Moses (drawn out) and all the Prophets, He explained to them what was written in all the Scriptures (Writings) about Himself.
24.28: As they approached the village where they were headed, He seemed to be going farther.
24.29: But they pleaded with Him, “Stay with us, for it is nearly evening and the day is almost over. So He went in to stay with them.
24.30: While He was reclining at the table with them, He took bread, spoke a blessing and broke it, and gave it to them.
24.31: Then their eyes were opened and they recognized Jesus—and He disappeared from their sight.
24.32: They asked each other, “Were not our hearts burning within us as He spoke with us on the road and opened the Scriptures (Writings) to us?
24.33: And they got up that very hour and returned to Jerusalem (city of peace). There they found the Eleven and those with them, gathered together
24.34: and saying, “The Lord {Kyrios} has indeed risen and has appeared to Simon (hearing)!
24.35: Then the two told what had happened on the road, and how they had recognized Jesus in the breaking of the bread.
24.36: While they were describing these events, Jesus Himself stood among them and said, “Peace be with you?.
24.37: But they were startled and frightened, thinking they had seen a spirit {pneuma}.
24.38: “Why are you? troubled, Jesus asked, “and why do doubts arise in your? hearts?
24.39: Look at (behold) My hands and My feet. It is I Myself. Touch Me and see—for a spirit {pneuma} does not have flesh and bones, as you? see I have.
24.40: And when He had said this, He showed them His hands and feet.
24.41: While they were still in disbelief because of their joy and amazement, He asked them, “Do you? have anything here to eat?
24.42: So they gave Him a piece of broiled fish [and some honeycomb.],
24.43: and He took it and ate it in front of them.
24.44: Jesus said to them, “These are the words I spoke to you? while I was still with you?: Everything must be fulfilled that is written about Me in the Law of Moses (drawn out), the Prophets, and the Psalms.
24.45: Then He opened their minds to understand the Scriptures (Writings).
24.46: And He told them, “This is what is written: The Christ (the Anointed One) will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day,
24.47: and in His name repentance and [repentance for] forgiveness of sins will be proclaimed to all nations {ethn?}, beginning in Jerusalem (city of peace).
24.48: You? are witnesses of these things.
24.49: And behold, I am sending the promise of My Father upon you?. But remain in the city until you? have been clothed with power {dynamin} from on high.
24.50: When Jesus had led them out as far as Bethany (house of affliction), He lifted up His hands and blessed them.
24.51: While He was blessing them, He left them and was carried up into heaven.
24.52: And they worshiped Him and returned to Jerusalem (city of peace) with great joy,
24.53: praising God {Theon} continually in the temple.
